body: I agree with the early comment that having [styles] return "None, None" if styles aren't in use, wasn't very elegant, so I instead made it return nothing. But then that exposed a problem when a commenter used `[prompt_no_styles] + [styles]` as a directory naming pattern. Running strip() on this line wouldn't have prevented the trailing space because it was built into the user's input. Returning "No styles" makes the issue moot (unless they had a trailing space in the style name, I guess?), and I think it is an overall more helpful return value if a user is using [styles] in their naming patterns.

body:
> Also, input before rearrange is batch, seq, head * dim, so we'd need to rearrange anyways to split heads, I think.

this is just a change in the strides of the tensor, and wouldn't require any data copy, so that should be fine in terms of performance.

> It'd run but return a broken image

Did you still split the head from the embedding when doing that? If your format is `B N (H*D)` this will be interpreted as `(B*H) N D` by xformers and produce the wrong output.
Could you print the following to help me debug things?
```
print("attention_op", self.attention_op)
print("dispatch", xformers.ops.AttentionOpDispatch.from_arguments(q, k, v))
print("dispatch_op", xformers.ops.AttentionOpDispatch.from_arguments(q, k, v).op)
```

Technically, *no* model requires a copy. The files are extracted from the target checkpoint when you create a new dreambooth model. They will reside on disk until training is completed and the datafolder for that model is deleted manually.

The only file that actually gets downloaded is the config file needed to load the model.

body:
说一下我的理解，如有错误请指出。
Denoising strength其实也是术语，特指在SD这里。这里的denoise其实并非常规的denoise（不是那种后处理滤镜）。而是同时指“加噪”强度（所以我一开始的翻译“模糊”是错的，那种只适合描述常规降噪滤镜）。在sd进行sampling的过程中，他要先有noise（高斯噪点）然后逐渐从noise“恢复”成图片。这个“恢复”的过程就叫denoise。而假设我们有1份noise（100%），step则决定每个step去除多少%的noise。比如100步就是每步1%。
i2i的生成过程就是，假如你的denoise强度是1.0，那他会加100%的噪点，然后跑100%的sampling。而假如denoise是0.0，那他就不加噪点，也就是不做sampling，直接encode进latent然后再直接decode回来。所以很自然0.5的denoise就只会跑一半的step，因为他只加了50%的noise。
所以其实在sd官方，这个参数不叫Denoising strength，而是直接叫strength（strength for noising/unnoising）
因此其实想靠翻译表达准确的含义是挺难的，我觉得以功能取代原有名字也是一种解决办法。

author: ArcticFaded

body:
The API is the very last thing to launch, all extensions and scripts are loaded before. Just create your endpoints and call `extendApi` when your script file is loaded and that's all.

For example, in `extensions/test-extension/scripts` create a python file with this code

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from modules.api.api import extendApi

subapi = FastAPI()

@subapi.get("/sub")
def read_sub():
    return {"message": "Hello World from sub API"}

extendApi("/subapi", subapi)
```

And that all you need, you can now start the server and go to `localhost:7860/subapi/docs` to see the documentation of your new endpoint.

> @Klace what do you think about naming it `.ip2p` rather than `.pix2pix`? My understanding is that the academic name for img2img is actually pix2pix but somehow it got named img2img in mainstream usage, so this "instruct" approach is just using the existing academic name "pix2pix" to mean the same thing as "img2img". So the "instruct" part is the novel aspect, not the "pix2pix" part. I'd therefore say it is rather confusing to call it `pix2pix`. Is it too late to change the file extension? I'm curious if you agree or not with this reasoning and consider it worth a change.

I am concerned that "instruct" as the keyword could cause issues, for instance if someone creates a model for "Instructional_Material.ckpt" or "Sexy Instructors.ckpt" just as some theoretical model names that someone could create. I agree using the special token to hijack isn't the best solution due to this. I was not aware of the original academic name for img2img being pix2pix (makes sense).

If we use a new unique name (i.e. ip2p) then users will have to rename the model when downloading from the creator. I suppose we can use the full "instruct-pix2pix" keyword instead, that would be the safest to me and supports the original model filename.

body:
Perhaps this is just me, but I don't see the benefit of having send seed/size options being respected everywhere _except_ pnginfo, pnginfo should be included in this with that setting enabled imo.

For example, in my use case I generate at a low resolution in bulk, then I would go through manually and find the generations that stand out and are worth exploring, edit the image in PS to fix hands or oddities, finally I then drag it into pnginfo, click send to img2img and then upscale the image to the correct resolution i need. This mimics the later part of txt2img with upscale fix exactly.

Without pnginfo honouring this setting each time I add a new image and send the gen params it uses the existing low resolution width and height, it also inserts the same seed the low res sample was generated with (using the same seed to upscale doesn't upscale correctly), so in order to avoid these you have to manually click random seed and edit the height and width each time. ...

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'astunparse'

author: vladmandic

body:
The TensorFlow pip packages should automatically install `astunparse` as a required dependency as listed in their package metadata and same with [setup.py](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/8faf71f12fdb6f75a06cc4455c19da7e2e1ab4ba/tensorflow/tools/pip_package/setup.py#L86) when installing from source.

```
Metadata-Version: 2.1
Name: tensorflow
Version: 2.12.0
...
Requires-Dist: astunparse (>=1.6.0)
...
```
```
Metadata-Version: 2.1
Name: tensorflow-intel
Version: 2.12.0
...
Requires-Dist: astunparse (>=1.6.0)
...
```

So this seems redundant when the webui base itself doesn't even install TensorFlow, only some extensions do. None the less, if you feel this is really needed, it would be wise to pin the same version range as TensorFlow itself.

body:
Do you think it makes sense to create a wrapper for a library call to Pillow's PIL.Image.open() ? It's already handling missing files and non-images as exceptions.

I would tend to think it should be the caller's responsibility to either making sure it's called with proper parameters (i.e. an existing file, or a proper image file) or catching any potential exception if they don't.

Depending on where it's called, some may want to do different things based on the context or which exceptions could be thrown. In this case here, we can skip the file. But it some other context we might want to do something else.

I see that in some places where Image.open() is used, the files are already checked for existence `(generation_parameters_copy_paste.image_from_url_text()`), or exceptions are being handled (`images.image_data`, the second use inside `postprocessing.run_postprocessing()`)

I see only two places where it's not necessarily handled (`ui.process_interrogate()` and the first occurrence inside `postprocessing.run_postprocessing()`) but I would tend to think these should be fixed there according to the context.

I'd rather fix these last two as well in the same PR and let each other caller fix their calls if needed (i.e. each extension takes care of input validation and/or calling properly and/or catching exceptions, since all is already handled by exceptions in `PIL.Image.open()`).
author: Z-nonymous

body: My rationale was that some image file types are not supported by PIL.Image (i.e. svg). If the webui eventually starts loading these too, then I think centralizing in a global function an image opening function would be a good idea as it means extensions dont have to hijack or reinvent the wheel.
author: ljleb

body:
Right, but ultimately, let's say we write a wrapper to open a file. Then it would return None if it's not a file.

- Caller would have to test for None as possible return anyway.
- Caller wouldn't know if it's because the file doesn't exist or if the format of the file is invalid.

I imagine you're talking about having a specific procedure to retrieve the images just for the case of using a source input directory (for instance get_images_from_directory(path_to_dir) that would return all the images that PIL.Images can open in that folder).

But the problem is that in some places the input is provided as a list of paths, sometimes as a name of a dir, sometimes as a dict of files, so this means refactoring some areas.
